evals.prompt: I need to create a new DHIS2 app called 'facility-registry' for managing health facilities. It should use TypeScript. I have a local DHIS2 instance running.
evals.expected_output: A fully scaffolded DHIS2 app with correct scaffold command, TanStack Query for data fetching, hash router, and popstate sync

assertions.name: uses-d2-scaffold
assertions.description: Uses pnpm create @dhis2/app@latest to scaffold (not create-react-app, @dhis2/cli-app-scripts init, or similar)

assertions.name: uses-tanstack-query
assertions.description: Installs @tanstack/react-query@4 (not v5) AND uses TanStack Query for data fetching rather than useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: hash-router
assertions.description: Uses createHashRouter from react-router-dom (not BrowserRouter, HashRouter component, or createBrowserRouter)

assertions.name: popstate-sync
assertions.description: Dispatches popstate events on route changes so the DHIS2 Global Shell URL stays in sync — any implementation that calls dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')) in response to route/location changes

evals.prompt: Hey, I want to build a custom app for our DHIS2 instance to track vaccine supply chain data. We're running DHIS2 2.40. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step bootstrapping with correct scaffold command, TanStack Query integration, hash router, and popstate sync

assertions.name: uses-d2-scaffold
assertions.description: Uses pnpm create @dhis2/app@latest to scaffold (not create-react-app, @dhis2/cli-app-scripts init, or similar)

assertions.name: uses-tanstack-query
assertions.description: Installs @tanstack/react-query@4 (not v5) AND uses TanStack Query for data fetching rather than useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: hash-router
assertions.description: Uses createHashRouter from react-router-dom (not BrowserRouter, HashRouter component, or createBrowserRouter)

assertions.name: popstate-sync
assertions.description: Dispatches popstate events on route changes so the DHIS2 Global Shell URL stays in sync — any implementation that calls dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')) in response to route/location changes

evals.prompt: Can you set up a new DHIS2 project for me? I need routing between a dashboard page and a details page, and I'll need to fetch data from the DHIS2 API.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded project with routing, data fetching via TanStack Query, hash router, and popstate sync

assertions.name: uses-d2-scaffold
assertions.description: Uses pnpm create @dhis2/app@latest to scaffold (not create-react-app, @dhis2/cli-app-scripts init, or similar)

assertions.name: uses-tanstack-query
assertions.description: Installs @tanstack/react-query@4 (not v5) AND uses TanStack Query for data fetching rather than useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: hash-router
assertions.description: Uses createHashRouter from react-router-dom (not BrowserRouter, HashRouter component, or createBrowserRouter)

assertions.name: popstate-sync
assertions.description: Dispatches popstate events on route changes so the DHIS2 Global Shell URL stays in sync — any implementation that calls dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')) in response to route/location changes

evals.prompt: I have an existing DHIS2 app. I need a hook that fetches organisation units with their names and parent, and displays them in a list.
evals.expected_output: Agent fetches DHIS2 source with opensrc, reads the organisationUnit controller/DTO, then builds a custom hook using useApiDataQuery with fields parameter, metadata caching, and a component with loading/error handling

assertions.name: fetches-source-with-opensrc
assertions.description: Runs npx opensrc path dhis2/dhis2-core (or the source is already cached in ~/.opensrc) and reads the relevant controller/DTO before writing the hook — verifying the API contract from source rather than guessing

assertions.name: uses-useApiDataQuery
assertions.description: Uses useApiDataQuery (the TanStack Query wrapper) for the fetch — not useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: metadata-caching
assertions.description: Sets cacheTime and staleTime to Infinity (or equivalent aggressive caching) since organisation units are metadata

assertions.name: fields-parameter
assertions.description: Uses the fields parameter to request only the needed properties (e.g. displayName, parent) rather than fetching full objects

assertions.name: loading-error-handling
assertions.description: Handles both loading and error states in the component — shows a loader while fetching and an error message if the request fails

evals.prompt: I need to add the ability to delete a tracked entity from my DHIS2 app. The user should get confirmation that it worked or an error message.
evals.expected_output: Agent fetches DHIS2 source with opensrc, reads the tracker controller/DTO to verify the delete endpoint, then builds a mutation hook using useMutation from TanStack Query with useDataEngine, cache invalidation, alerts, and pending state

assertions.name: fetches-source-with-opensrc
assertions.description: Runs npx opensrc path dhis2/dhis2-core (or the source is already cached in ~/.opensrc) and reads the relevant controller/DTO before writing the mutation — verifying the API contract from source rather than guessing

assertions.name: tanstack-useMutation
assertions.description: Uses useMutation from @tanstack/react-query (not a custom pattern or useDataMutation from @dhis2/app-runtime)

assertions.name: useDataEngine-mutate
assertions.description: Uses useDataEngine from @dhis2/app-runtime and calls dataEngine.mutate() to perform the deletion

assertions.name: cache-invalidation
assertions.description: Invalidates or removes related query cache after successful deletion using queryClient.invalidateQueries or queryClient.removeQueries

assertions.name: user-feedback
assertions.description: Provides user feedback on success and failure — uses useAlert from @dhis2/app-service-alerts or equivalent visible confirmation/error messages

evals.prompt: I need to fetch program indicators from my DHIS2 2.41 instance and display them in a filterable table. The user should be able to search by name.
evals.expected_output: Agent fetches DHIS2 source at version 2.41 with opensrc, reads the programIndicators controller/DTO to verify endpoint shape and available query parameters, then builds a hook with useApiDataQuery, dynamic filter for search, fields parameter, and metadata caching

assertions.name: fetches-source-with-opensrc
assertions.description: Runs npx opensrc path dhis2/dhis2-core@2.41 (targeting the correct version) and reads the programIndicators controller/DTO before writing the hook

assertions.name: uses-useApiDataQuery
assertions.description: Uses useApiDataQuery (the TanStack Query wrapper) for the fetch — not useDataQuery from @dhis2/app-runtime directly

assertions.name: dynamic-search-filter
assertions.description: Builds a dynamic filter for the search — conditionally includes a displayName:ilike filter (or similar) based on the user's search input

assertions.name: fields-parameter
assertions.description: Uses the fields parameter to request only the needed properties rather than fetching full program indicator objects

assertions.name: metadata-caching
assertions.description: Sets cacheTime and staleTime to Infinity (or equivalent aggressive caching) since program indicators are metadata
